text: Dragonfly's Haseeb: Annualized DeFi Hack Losses for 2026 Remain Below 2025 Levels Despite AI Threats Annualized losses from DeFi hacks in 2026 remain below 2025 levels and within the historical range, Dragonfly managing partner Haseeb Qureshi said, arguing that fears of an AI-driven DeFi "hackpocalypse" have not materialized. Although the number of incidents has risen, the median loss per hack has continued to fall, indicating that attackers are increasingly targeting smaller or abandoned protocols. Losses from admin-key and multisig compromises have also declined, while larger protocols appear more resilient after strengthening their defenses against AI-enabled attacks. — link

text: How to break the memory supercycle? Or is it even possible? The Big 4 DRAM makers are projected to grow capacity from ~2M WPM in 2026 to 4.8M WPM by 2030 through major fab expansions AI-driven demand is forecast to reach 157.5 EB by 2030. After accounting for HBM's 4x wafer multiplier and node/yield factors, this creates a ~25% shortfall in general DRAM supply, keeping ASPs high (DRAM $1.5–2.0/Gb, HBM $5–6/Gb) and pushing the total DRAM market to ~$3 trillion

text: L2Beat Says RAIN Token Appears "Heavily Manipulated" After Removing $7B of Team-Controlled Supply From Arbitrum TVS ​​​L2Beat said it has removed around $7 billion worth of non-circulating RAIN tokens held in team-controlled multisigs from Arbitrum's Total Value Secured (TVS) metric. Despite the adjustment, RAIN still accounts for around $2.6 billion in TVS, making it the largest asset on Arbitrum, surpassing USDC and ETH. L2Beat researchers described the situation as "clearly absurd" and said the token appears to be "heavily manipulated," adding that further investigation is underway. RAIN is a prediction market protocol built on Arbitrum. The project announced a $100 million liquidity commitment in May and briefly became one of the three largest prediction market protocols by valuation. — link

text: QCP Market Colour, 13 July 2026 Crypto enters the week with little conviction as markets brace for a packed calendar of macro and corporate catalysts. Tuesday's US CPI release and Fed Chair Warsh's two-day testimony before Congress are likely to shape expectations for the Fed's policy path, with investors looking for greater clarity on the outlook for rates. The macro calendar is only half the story. Q2 earnings season begins this week with the major US banks, followed by several blue-chip names across technology and financials. With equity valuations already elevated, particularly across AI infrastructure, companies may need to deliver more than earnings beats to keep the rally intact. For crypto, the broader backdrop remains constructive, supported by institutional adoption and steady ETF demand. However, without a clearer macro catalyst, BTC may remain rangebound as markets wait for greater conviction. Is this week's CPI and earnings season enough to reignite risk appetite, or will markets continue to wait for a clearer signal from the Fed? Read the full Market Colour here.

text: South Korea's KOSPI Closes Down 8.95% After Circuit Breaker Triggered, SK Hynix Falls Over 15% On July 13, South Korea's KOSPI triggered a circuit breaker during intraday trading and closed down 8.95% at 6,806.93. SK Hynix fell 15.37% to KRW 1.845 million, down about 38% from its intraday record high on June 25. CoinGecko data shows that Upbit, South Korea's largest crypto exchange, recorded approximately $4.23 billion in trading volume over the past 24 hours, up 550.3%. Bithumb, the country's second-largest crypto exchange, recorded approximately $785 million, up 324.4%. — link

text: Spot Bitcoin ETFs Record $197 Million in Weekly Net Inflows, Ending Eight-Week Outflow Streak From July 6 to 10 (ET), U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $197 million in net inflows, ending an eight-week outflow streak. Spot Ethereum ETFs saw $84.42 million in net inflows, also ending eight consecutive weeks of outflows. Spot SOL ETFs drew $930,400, HYPE ETFs $10.36 million, while XRP ETFs posted $7.18 million in net outflows. — link

text: Japan PM Sanae Takaichi Reaffirms Startup and Web3 Support at WebX 2026 Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi said in a video address at WebX 2026 that the government will strengthen startup support through measures including increased funding from government-backed funds and financial institutions and regulatory easing. She said the government's initiatives and WebX could jointly support the development of Japan's Web3 innovation ecosystem. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: American Bitcoin, Co-Founded by Eric Trump, Plunges More Than 95% From Peak Bloomberg reported that American Bitcoin shares have fallen over 95% from their peak, wiping more than $600 million from the value of Eric Trump's roughly 6% stake over the past 10 months. The company conducted a 1-for-15 reverse stock split to maintain its Nasdaq listing and hit a record low on Wednesday. It continues to mine and accumulate Bitcoin, adding 500 BTC on Monday to bring holdings above 8,000 BTC. In Q1, it posted a $118.2 million operating loss after a $117.2 million Bitcoin impairment. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: Japan's Top-Three Convenience Store Chain Lawson to Launch Country's First POS-Integrated Stablecoin Payment Trial Japan's top-three convenience store chain Lawson will trial yen-denominated stablecoin JPYC payments in early August at its Takanawa Gateway City store in Tokyo, in partnership with HashPort. Customers will pay via mobile wallet barcodes. Lawson said it will be Japan's first stablecoin payment trial integrated with a POS system. — link
